meta_title: Impact Doors in Weston, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Weston, FL. Protect your home in Weston Hills, Savanna & more. Free quotes, financing from $87/mo.
h1: Impact Doors for Weston Homes Built to Withstand South Florida's Fiercest Storms
intro:
Weston is one of Broward County's most coveted communities — a master-planned city where tree-lined streets wind through neighborhoods like Weston Hills, Savanna, The Ridges, Windmill Ranch Estates, and Emerald Estates, and where the average home value sits at a well-earned $550,000. For homeowners who have invested significantly in their properties, protecting that investment from South Florida's unpredictable hurricane seasons isn't optional — it's essential. Impact doors represent one of the single most effective upgrades a Weston homeowner can make, combining robust storm protection with everyday security, energy efficiency, and long-term property value enhancement.

Weston sits approximately 15 miles inland from the Atlantic coast, which can create a false sense of security during hurricane season. The truth is that inland position offers only modest protection; wind speeds in Weston's 140 mph wind zone remain powerful enough to turn an unprotected entry door or sliding glass door into a catastrophic failure point during a major storm. When a door fails under hurricane-force pressure, it's not just the door itself that suffers — the sudden breach in your home's envelope allows wind pressure to build rapidly inside, threatening the roof structure and exposing your entire interior to rain and debris. Impact doors are engineered specifically to prevent this chain reaction, keeping your home sealed and structurally sound from the first gust to the last.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we understand the specific needs of Weston homeowners. Whether you're refreshing a sprawling estate near Windmill Ranch Estates or upgrading a family home steps from Weston Regional Park, our team brings 60+ years of combined installation experience and 500+ completed South Florida homes to every project. We install ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI impact door systems — premium brands that meet or exceed Florida Building Code requirements for Broward County's 140 mph wind zone. From elegant impact-rated entry doors to expansive sliding glass doors and French door configurations, we have the products and expertise to protect your Weston home without compromising its curb appeal.

section_why.h2: Why Weston Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Impact Door Installation
section_why.body:
Choosing an impact door contractor in Weston isn't simply a matter of selecting a product off a shelf — it's about partnering with a team that understands South Florida's building environment, local permitting processes, and the unique architectural character of Broward County's most prestigious neighborhoods. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation as 'The Kings of Service' by delivering on every element of that promise: expert consultation, precision installation, flawless permit management, and post-installation support that keeps our clients confident long after the job is done.

One of the most important distinctions that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart is our 100% in-house installation model. We do not subcontract our work to third-party crews. Every impact door we install in Weston — whether it's a grand double-door entryway on a Savanna estate or a multi-panel sliding glass door overlooking a pool in The Ridges — is handled by our own trained, licensed, and insured installation specialists. This means consistent quality, direct accountability, and a level of craftsmanship that subcontracted labor simply cannot guarantee. Our installers are intimately familiar with the City of Weston Building Division's permitting requirements, ensuring that every project is fully permitted, inspected, and code-compliant.

Our A+ BBB Rating and portfolio of 500+ completed homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties speak to a track record that Weston homeowners can rely on. We take time during the consultation process to assess your home's specific door openings, existing framing conditions, and architectural style before recommending the right ESW or CWI impact door system for your needs. Many of our Weston clients are surprised to discover how dramatically impact doors can transform the look and feel of their entryways — adding elegance and security simultaneously. From the first phone call to the final inspection, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages every step so you don't have to, making the entire process as smooth and stress-free as possible.

section_history.h2: Weston's Storm History Makes the Case for Impact Doors
section_history.body:
Weston and the surrounding Broward County region have experienced eight significant tropical systems since 1990, and three storms in particular have left lasting marks on the community's understanding of hurricane preparedness. Hurricane Andrew in 1992, though its most devastating strike was centered in Miami-Dade County, sent powerful outer bands across Broward County that shook homes and underscored the vulnerability of standard residential doors throughout South Florida. The storm fundamentally changed how Florida regulates construction — and Weston's post-Andrew building stock reflects the stricter codes that followed.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 was perhaps the most instructive storm for Weston residents. Wilma crossed South Florida rapidly, with sustained winds that caused widespread structural damage across Broward County. Homes with standard doors experienced failures that allowed rain infiltration and interior damage costing tens of thousands of dollars per property. By contrast, homes equipped with impact-rated doors and windows performed dramatically better, with significantly fewer interior losses. Then Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced those lessons again — delivering sustained tropical storm to hurricane-force winds across Broward County and reminding even newer Weston residents that storm seasons in South Florida demand year-round vigilance.

About 15% of Weston's housing stock predates 1994 — before the post-Andrew building code overhaul took effect. These older homes are statistically the most vulnerable during storm events and represent the most urgent candidates for impact door upgrades. Even for homes built in the late 1990s and 2000s, original door systems are now approaching the end of their effective service life and may no longer meet current code standards. With storm season an annual reality for every ZIP code in Weston — 33326, 33327, 33331, and 33332 — proactive upgrading to impact-rated doors is not just smart; it's the responsible choice for any homeowner who values their property and their family's safety.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code and Broward County Requirements for Impact Doors in Weston
section_code.body:
Weston falls within Broward County's wind speed jurisdiction of 140 mph, which places it under stringent Florida Building Code requirements for all exterior openings, including entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors. While Weston is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — the most restrictive designation covering Miami-Dade and parts of Broward closer to the coast — the city still requires that all hurricane-protection products meet the Florida Building Code's minimum impact resistance standards. This means every impact door installed in a Weston home must carry valid Product Approval documentation demonstrating it has been tested to withstand the area's design wind loads.

The City of Weston Building Division oversees the permitting and inspection process for all impact door installations within city limits. Homeowners are required to pull permits before work begins, and installations must be inspected by the city's building inspectors to confirm code compliance. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on behalf of our clients — preparing documentation, submitting applications, coordinating inspection schedules, and delivering a fully closed permit at project completion. This protects homeowners not only from code violations but also from potential complications during future home sales or insurance claims.

Product approval under the Florida Building Code is non-negotiable. Both ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI impact door systems installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing carry the necessary Florida Product Approvals for use in Broward County's 140 mph wind zone. Beyond impact resistance, these systems must also meet water infiltration and air infiltration standards under the code, ensuring that your doors seal tightly against both wind-driven rain and everyday energy loss. When you work with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you can be confident that every door we install in Weston is fully code-compliant, properly permitted, and built to perform when South Florida's storms arrive.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Protection for Weston's Distinctive Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading impact door brands in Weston: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both product lines are engineered specifically for South Florida's demanding climate and carry the certifications required for installation in Broward County's 140 mph wind zone. Whether your Weston home features a traditional Mediterranean-style entryway, a contemporary glass-panel design, or a sweeping pool-view sliding door configuration, ESW and CWI offer the performance ratings, glass options, and aesthetic versatility to match your home's character while delivering uncompromising protection.

ESW's impact door systems are known for their robust construction and precise engineering. Their entry door line features heavy-duty aluminum or steel frames paired with laminated impact glass, multi-point locking systems that engage at multiple points along the door frame for superior security and structural integrity, and a variety of panel designs and glass configurations. For Weston homes in communities like Emerald Estates or Windmill Ranch Estates where curb appeal is a point of pride, ESW doors offer the aesthetic refinement that high-value properties deserve. CWI impact doors complement this lineup with an equally strong performance profile, offering excellent energy efficiency ratings alongside their impact credentials — a meaningful benefit for Weston homeowners looking to reduce cooling costs in South Florida's relentless heat.

For sliding glass doors and French door configurations — common in Weston's spacious single-family homes — both ESW and CWI provide multi-panel systems that maintain clean sightlines and smooth operation without sacrificing storm performance. Multi-point locking hardware engages along the full height of the door frame, dramatically improving resistance to forced entry as well as wind pressure. Impact-laminated glass in these systems is designed to remain intact even when struck by wind-driven debris, preventing dangerous shattering and maintaining the integrity of your home's envelope throughout a storm event.

section_cost.h2: Impact Door Costs and Financing Options for Weston Homeowners
section_cost.body:
For a Weston home with an average value of $550,000, impact door installation represents a highly efficient home improvement investment — one that pays dividends in protection, energy savings, insurance discounts, and long-term property value. The cost of impact door projects in Weston varies depending on the type and size of door, the number of openings being upgraded, and specific site conditions such as framing requirements or threshold modifications. Entry door replacements typically begin in the range of $2,000 to $4,500 per unit installed, while larger sliding glass door systems or custom French door configurations can range from $4,000 to $10,000 or more depending on panel count and glass specifications.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes impact door upgrades accessible to Weston homeowners through flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month, with $0 down programs available for qualified applicants. When you consider that a single impact door project can yield annual insurance savings of several hundred dollars or more — plus eliminate the need to purchase, store, and deploy hurricane shutters each storm season — the real cost of waiting often exceeds the cost of acting now. Our team provides detailed, transparent quotes during the consultation process so you can evaluate the full financial picture before committing.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact doors in Weston's high-value real estate market are a compelling upgrade. Real estate professionals across Broward County consistently note that impact-protected homes command premium pricing and faster sales compared to equivalent properties without storm protection. For homeowners in communities like Weston Hills, Savanna, or The Ridges, where buyers expect a certain standard of quality and security, having fully impact-protected doors and windows can be a decisive factor in sale negotiations. The upgrade essentially pays for itself multiple times over across insurance savings, avoided storm damage, energy efficiency gains, and enhanced resale value.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Credits for Weston Impact Door Upgrades
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate financial benefits of installing impact doors in Weston is the potential for significant homeowner's insurance premium reductions. Florida's insurance market is among the most expensive in the nation, and Broward County policyholders have seen premiums rise substantially in recent years as carriers reassess their risk exposure in the region. Impact doors, as part of a broader wind mitigation strategy, are among the most recognized upgrades by insurance underwriters — and they can translate directly into meaningful annual savings on your policy.

Florida law requires insurance companies to offer wind mitigation discounts to homeowners who can demonstrate that their property meets certain storm-protection standards. A wind mitigation inspection — conducted by a licensed inspector — documents the presence of impact-rated opening protections, including doors, windows, and garage doors. When impact doors are installed across all or most of a home's exterior openings in Weston, the wind mitigation report reflects a more favorable risk profile, which insurers are required to credit against the windstorm portion of your premium. For high-value Weston homes carrying substantial replacement cost policies, these credits can represent savings of $500 to $2,000 or more annually depending on your carrier and coverage level.

Major carriers operating in Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Property & Casualty — all recognize wind mitigation credits for impact-rated opening protections. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides clients with all product approval documentation needed to support a wind mitigation inspection and insurance filing following installation. We also encourage Weston homeowners to proactively contact their insurance agent before and after installation to ensure they capture every available discount. When combined with the elimination of hurricane shutter costs and the avoidance of deductible-triggering storm damage, the insurance savings from impact doors in Weston create a compelling long-term financial case for upgrading today.

faqs.q: Do Weston homes really need impact doors if they're 15 miles from the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Weston sits in a 140 mph wind zone under Florida Building Code, meaning the area is engineered to withstand significant hurricane-force winds regardless of coastal distance. Inland storms like Hurricane Wilma demonstrated that Weston homes face real wind damage risk. Standard doors are among the weakest points in a home's structure during a storm — impact doors eliminate that vulnerability entirely, protecting both the home's interior and its structural envelope.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Weston, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in Weston typically range from $2,000 to $4,500 per entry door and $4,000 to $10,000+ for larger sliding glass or French door systems, depending on size and configuration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified applicants. We provide transparent, itemized quotes during consultation so you can plan your investment with full confidence.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to install impact doors in Weston, and who handles that process?
faqs.a: Yes — all impact door installations in Weston require permits through the City of Weston Building Division. Permits must be issued before work begins, and completed installations are subject to city inspection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf, from application submission to final inspection closeout. Our team is thoroughly familiar with Broward County and Weston's specific code requirements, ensuring a smooth, fully compliant project from start to finish.

faqs.q: How long does an impact door installation take in Weston?
faqs.a: Most residential impact door installations in Weston are completed within one to two days once permits are approved. The permitting process typically takes one to three weeks depending on city workload. During consultation, our team will provide a realistic project timeline specific to your home's scope and the current permitting queue at the City of Weston Building Division. We schedule all work at your convenience and keep you informed at every stage of the process.

faqs.q: Will impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance in Weston?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Florida law requires insurers to provide wind mitigation discounts to homeowners with documented impact-rated opening protections. A licensed wind mitigation inspector will document your impact doors following installation, and that report is submitted to your carrier to trigger available credits on the windstorm portion of your premium. For Weston's high-value homes, annual savings can range from several hundred to over $1,000 depending on your policy, carrier, and the scope of impact protection installed.
